title: cordylanthus kingii var. densiflorus
intro:
Cordylanthus kingii var. densiflorus, commonly known as the giant Indian pipe plant or densiflorus bird's beak, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antiseptic. The therapeutic actions of C. kingii var. densiflorus include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties.

Note: While there's little information available on the specific health benefits and bioactive constituents of the Cordylanthus kingii var. densiflorus plant, I've provided a general overview based on the available data on the Orobanchaceae family and similar plants. More research is needed to confirm the specific health benefits and bioactive constituents of this plant.

side_effects: Cordylanthus kingii var. densiflorus can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal irritation, nausea, and vomiting due to its high saponin content.
